status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

features.title: Electron window starts successfully
features.user_visible_behavior: Running the app opens a desktop window titled Knowledge Base at 1200×800.
verification: Run npm run dev from the project root.
Verify a BrowserWindow opens without crashing.
Verify the window is approximately 1200×800 and shows the app title.
Verify contextIsolation is enabled and nodeIntegration is disabled.
evidence: npm run dev launches without error after unsetting ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE in scripts/dev.js
main.ts: BrowserWindow 1200x800, title Knowledge Base, contextIsolation true, nodeIntegration false

features.title: UI shows the document-list area
features.user_visible_behavior: The left sidebar displays a Documents section with an empty state or imported document cards.
verification: Launch the app with npm run dev.
Verify a left sidebar labeled Documents is visible.
With no imports, verify an empty-state message is shown.
After documents exist, verify document cards appear in the list.
evidence: App.tsx renders left sidebar with Documents ({count}) header and DocumentList component
DocumentList.tsx shows empty state: No documents imported yet.

features.title: UI shows the QA panel
features.user_visible_behavior: A question input and Ask button appear at the bottom; submitting sends the question via IPC.
verification: Launch the app with npm run dev.
Verify a text input and Ask button are visible at the bottom of the window.
Type a question and press Enter or click Ask.
Verify the question is submitted through window.knowledgeBase.qa.ask without console errors.
evidence: QuestionPanel.tsx renders input + Ask button; submits via onAsk handler
App.tsx handleAskQuestion calls window.knowledgeBase.qa.ask(question) via preload IPC

features.title: App creates and uses a local data directory
features.user_visible_behavior: On startup the app creates and reads from a knowledge-base-data folder under userData.
verification: Launch the app once with npm run dev.
Verify required subdirectories (e.g. documents, index) are created.
Verify the app uses this directory for read/write operations without errors.
evidence: PersistenceService.ensureDirectories creates dataDir, documents, and index subdirs
Runtime check: new PersistenceService(tmp) created documents/ and index/ successfully
